text.title: LinkedIn Profile Optimization and Content Strategy
text.summary: Profile elements that convert visitors to followers, headline and About section formulas, and the five content types with post structure that drives LinkedIn organic reach.

rule.statement: Treat the LinkedIn profile as a landing page: every element (headline, photo, banner, About, Featured, Experience) must reinforce a single value proposition.
rule.rationale: Content drives traffic; the profile converts it. A weak headline wastes all the impressions that posts generate — visitors arrive, see no clear value, and leave without following.

rule.statement: Write headlines using the formula: [What you do] | [For whom] | [Proof or differentiator] — not just a job title.
rule.rationale: Job titles describe employment; headlines must answer "what's in it for me?" for the visitor. Proof elements (numbers, exits, brands) signal credibility before the visitor reads further.

example.code: "Helping SaaS founders hit $1M ARR | 3x exits | DM me 'growth'" "Building AI tools for marketers | CEO @ProductName | ex-Google" "Writing about product strategy | 50K newsletter | Author of [Book]"

rule.statement: Anchor content to personal stories, contrarian takes, and how-to posts — not company announcements or generic industry news.
rule.rationale: LinkedIn's algorithm surfaces posts with early engagement. Personal and opinionated content generates comments in the first hour; announcements and news do not. Organic reach for personal-voice posts is 3-5x higher than corporate-voice posts.

list.item: Personal stories: vulnerability + concrete lesson from experience
Contrarian takes: challenge a common belief with evidence
How-to posts: numbered steps with one actionable takeaway each
Lessons learned: "After N years in X, here's what I know"
Carousels: visual guides with one point per slide (highest save rate)

section.title: Post Structure

rule.statement: Write the first line of every post as if it is the only line the reader will see — LinkedIn truncates after 2-3 lines; the hook must earn the "see more" click.
rule.rationale: 80% of LinkedIn feed readers never click "see more." If the first line does not create a question or tension, the post receives no engagement from that 80%.

rule.statement: Do not put URLs in the body of LinkedIn posts — put them in the first comment.
rule.rationale: LinkedIn's algorithm suppresses posts with external links in the body, reducing organic reach by 40-60%.

list.item: Value/Lesson: the insight or actionable takeaway
CTA: a question or simple call to action

section.reference: Story post template with hook-gap-lesson-CTA structure

rule.statement: Post 3-5 text posts per week and 1-2 carousels per week; comment on 20+ posts daily — the commenting compounds faster than standalone posts for early-stage accounts.
rule.rationale: Comments on high-follower posts (50K+) drive more profile visits than an equivalent standalone post, because the comment is seen by the original poster's entire audience.

list.item: Best times: 7-8 AM, 12 PM, 5-6 PM in audience timezone
